200.q: What city does Wonder Woman come from?
What is the name of Batman's company?
What city does Clark Kent work in for the Daily Planet?
What word does Billy Batson say to transform?

200.a: Superman's arch-nemesis — brilliant but ruthless billionaire

200.q: Who plays Batman in the Nolan trilogy?

200.a: The Lasso of Truth — and Amazonian sword and shield
A large searchlight in Gotham used to summon Batman
The ruler of Apokolips — DC's most powerful villain, seeking the Anti-Life Equation
Jerry Siegel and Joe Shuster — first appeared in Action Comics #1 in 1938
Victor Stone — athlete whose body was partially replaced with cybernetic technology
Radioactive material from Superman's home planet — weakens and can kill him
Selina Kyle — a skilled thief with a complicated romantic history with Batman
Dick Grayson — the original Robin who grew up and became his own hero
A villain obsessed with plants using pheromones and toxins — often linked with Harley Quinn
DC's superhero team — Batman, Superman, Wonder Woman, Flash, Aquaman, Cyborg, Green Lantern
Bruce Wayne's secret underground headquarters beneath Wayne Manor

200.q: Who is Harley Quinn's love interest?

200.a: The ruthless government official who runs Task Force X (Suicide Squad)
Billy Batson — a young boy who transforms into a superhero by saying SHAZAM!

400.q: What yellow weakness affects Green Lantern power rings?
400.a: Fear

400.q: Who is the main sidekick turned independent hero known as Nightwing?
400.a: Dick Grayson

400.q: What is the name of Superman's bottled city from Krypton?
400.a: Kandor

400.q: What is the name of Batman's armored vehicle in The Dark Knight trilogy?
400.a: The Tumbler

400.q: What planet does Darkseid rule in DC films and shows?
400.a: Apokolips

400.q: What 1984 artifact grants wishes in Wonder Woman 1984?
400.a: The Dreamstone

400.q: What is Harley Quinn's real first name?
400.a: Harleen

400.q: What prison houses dangerous criminals in The Dark Knight?
400.a: Arkham Asylum

400.q: What weapon does Harley Quinn famously use?
400.a: Baseball bat

400.q: What is Black Adam's human title in Kahndaq?
400.a: Champion

400.q: What is Wonder Woman's civilian name?
400.a: Diana Prince

400.q: What is Batman's real first name?
400.a: Bruce

400.q: Who is the main villain in Justice League (2017)?
400.a: Steppenwolf
Barry Allen — a forensic scientist
A dimensional prison used by Kryptonians — created by Jor-El
To find the Anti-Life Equation and control all sentient life in the universe

400.q: What is the Green Lantern Corps?
400.a: An intergalactic police force created by the Guardians of the Universe on Oa
A unique extradimensional energy that all speedsters draw power from
An alien villain who miniaturizes and collects cities from across the universe
Eobard Thawne — a future villain who killed Barry Allen's mother
A 2011 DC initiative that rebooted the entire DC Universe into a new continuity
A powerful sorceress in the Justice League — she speaks spells backwards
A DC storyline where dead heroes and villains are reanimated as Black Lanterns who consume emotion
Slade Wilson — one of DC's most formidable assassins, with enhanced abilities and tactical genius
Superman becomes a dictator after the Joker kills Lois Lane — Batman leads a resistance
J'onn J'onzz — a Martian superhero with telepathy, shapeshifting, and strength
A mathematical formula Darkseid seeks that would give him control over all sentient life

400.q: What is Crisis on Infinite Earths?
400.a: A 1985 DC storyline collapsing the multiverse — killing the original Supergirl and Barry Allen

400.q: What is the New Gods concept?
400.a: Jack Kirby's Fourth World mythology — beings from New Genesis and Apokolips representing good and evil
An ancient yellow fear entity that can possess Green Lanterns — it possessed Hal Jordan

400.q: What is the significance of Watchmen for DC?
400.a: A 1986-87 deconstruction of the superhero genre — set the template for darker superhero stories
A fifth-dimensional imp only defeated by tricking him into saying his name backwards — a Superman villain
An ancient Egyptian anti-hero with powers similar to Shazam — played by Dwayne Johnson in 2022

400.q: What is the significance of the Death of Superman?
400.a: Superman is killed by Doomsday in 1992 — a landmark event that became major news
Barry goes back to save his mother — creating an alternate timeline that led to the New 52 reboot
Commissioner Gordon's daughter — Batgirl, then Oracle after being shot by the Joker
Vic Sage — a faceless detective vigilante who inspired Watchmen's Rorschach
A 2005 DC storyline involving conflicts between alternate Earths and a rogue Superboy-Prime
A parallel universe with older Golden Age versions of DC heroes
The third Robin — often considered the most intelligent, who deduced Batman's identity as a child

400.q: What is the Tower of Babel storyline?
400.a: Batman's files are stolen — revealing his contingency plans to defeat every Justice League member
The cosmic mother of the multiverse — a villain in Scott Snyder's Justice League run

600.q: What is the antihero team led by Amanda Waller?
600.a: The Suicide Squad / Task Force X

600.q: What world does Darkseid rule?
600.a: Apokolips

600.q: What is the name of the evil Justice League from Earth-3?
600.a: The Crime Syndicate

600.q: What prison holds the captured metahumans in Arrow season 7?
600.a: Slabside

600.q: What event unites the Justice League against Steppenwolf in Zack Snyder's Justice League?
600.a: The Unity

600.q: What is Peacemaker's surname?
600.a: Smith

600.q: What nation does Black Adam rule?
600.a: Kahndaq

600.q: What body is Doomsday created from in Batman v Superman?
600.a: General Zod

600.q: What codename does Billy Batson's foster brother Freddy admire?
600.a: Captain Every Power

600.q: What prison island holds villains in The Suicide Squad?
600.a: Corto Maltese

600.q: What creature kills Superman in Batman v Superman?
600.a: Doomsday

600.q: What is the shark-headed squad member called?
600.a: King Shark

600.q: What city does Shazam protect with his foster family?
600.a: Philadelphia

600.q: What is Harley Quinn's surname?
600.a: Quinzel

600.q: What city is Arthur Curry tied to on land?
600.a: Amnesty Bay

600.q: What mercenary leads the team in The Suicide Squad?
600.a: Bloodsport

600.q: What villain does Robert Pattinson's Batman hunt with ciphers?
600.a: Riddler

600.q: What is the Doomsday Clock storyline?
600.a: Watchmen characters (especially Dr. Manhattan) cross into the main DC universe — connecting both canons

600.q: Pre-Crisis vs Post-Crisis Superman?
600.a: Pre-Crisis: omnipotent, could move planets. Post-Crisis (1985): more human and relatable

600.q: What is the Omega Effect?
600.a: Darkseid's signature power — energy beams pursuing their target across dimensions and time

600.q: What is the significance of No Man's Land in Batman?
600.a: Gotham is cut off after a massive earthquake — an entire year of Batman comics in a lawless isolated city

600.q: Who is Swamp Thing?
600.a: Alec Holland — a scientist reborn as a plant elemental, connected to the Green (force of all plant life)

600.q: What is Grant Morrison's contribution to Batman?
600.a: Morrison introduced all Batman stories as canon — including 1950s sci-fi stories — as psychological trauma

600.q: What is the Red Son storyline?
600.a: An Elseworlds tale where Superman's rocket lands in Soviet Ukraine instead of Kansas

600.q: What is the Sinestro Corps War?
600.a: Sinestro creates his own fear-wielding corps — the first major GL crossover event

600.q: Who is Seagrin in the New Gods?
600.a: One of the Forever People — the New Gods' young team who seek adventure on Earth

600.q: What is the significance of The Long Halloween?
600.a: A 13-issue Batman story by Jeph Loeb depicting his early career and the rise of Two-Face

600.q: What is the Knightfall storyline?
600.a: Bane breaks Batman's back — Jean-Paul Valley (Azrael) takes over as Batman before Bruce recovers

600.q: Who is Geo-Force?
600.a: Brion Markov — a prince with earth-manipulation powers, member of the Outsiders

600.q: What is the significance of Final Crisis?
600.a: Darkseid achieves the Anti-Life Equation and enslaves the Earth — Batman shoots Darkseid with a god-killing bullet

600.q: Who is Ultra-Man?
600.a: The evil Superman of Earth-3 — a member of the Crime Syndicate

600.q: What is the significance of Hypertime?
600.a: A DC concept allowing all previously published DC stories to be technically canon in branching realities

600.q: Who is Arion?
600.a: An ancient Atlantean sorcerer from 45,000 years ago — one of DC's oldest heroes

600.q: What is the significance of Superman's death and resurrection?
600.a: His death in 1992 was unprecedented — it caused media frenzy. His four replacements explored what Superman means

600.q: What is the Nth metal in DC Comics?
600.a: A mysterious metal from Thanagar that defies physics — worn by Hawkman in his anti-gravity harness

600.q: Who is Bat-Mite?
600.a: A fifth-dimensional imp who idolizes Batman and often causes chaos while trying to help him

600.q: What is the significance of Crisis on Infinite Earths vs Infinite Crisis?
600.a: CoIE (1985): destroyed the multiverse, created one universe. IC (2005): the multiverse reasserted itself
